sanskrit:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः।
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम्॥
transliteration:
samo ’haṃ sarva-bhūteṣu na me dveṣyo ’sti na priyaḥ |
ye bhajanti tu māṃ bhaktyā mayi te teṣu cāpy aham ||
literal_meaning: Equal am I to all beings; no one is hateful to Me nor dear. Yet those who worship Me with devotion are in Me, and I am also in them.
contextual_meaning: The psyche’s deepest security: the Self (Īśvara) is unconditionally accepting, but when ego turns toward it with sustained devotion, a reciprocal field of indwelling awareness is activated—like a mirror that only reflects when faced.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to the archetypal Good Parent; reduction of splitting (ideal vs. devalued self) through internalization of an equanimous inner presence.
trigger_scenario: A client who grew up with conditional praise from parents discovers in therapy that their inner critic softens when they practice daily contemplative prayer or mantra—experiencing that the ‘observer self’ neither favors nor rejects them, yet responds to consistent heartfelt attention.
anchor_text: Turn toward the Self with steady love and find it already within you.
